title: What just happened — INC-0721
question: Why did shared catalog links render a blank list, and what actually fixed it?
inputs: src/app.mjs — render glue at the incident revision
tldr: After the slice-4 deploy, links carrying a tag no skill has (?tag=deploy) rendered an empty, unexplained list. The render glue treated "unknown tag" and "no tag" as the same empty array and had no empty-state branch. A hotfix fell back to the full catalog for unknown tags; the durable fix — the accepted explain-and-suggest empty state — landed through interface-directions and STOP-01 the next day.

timeline.title: Slice 4 deploys the browser glue
timeline.body: renderFromUrl() starts re-deriving the catalog DOM from location.search on every load.

timeline.title: Impact starts — first blank-list report
timeline.body: A maintainer shares ?tag=deploy in chat; recipients see an empty list with no explanation. More shared links follow the same shape.

timeline.title: First hypothesis: the deploy broke rendering
timeline.body: Rolling back slice 4 locally reproduces a working catalog — but only because the static page has no filtering at all. The hypothesis is falsified: known tags render correctly on the deployed build.

timeline.title: Cause isolated to the unknown-tag branch
timeline.body: filterByTag returns [] for tags no skill carries, and renderFromUrl passes the empty array straight to replaceChildren — a blank <ul> with zero affordances. "No tag" and "unknown tag" collapse into the same render.

timeline.title: Hotfix: unknown tags fall back to the full catalog
timeline.body: A guard renders the unfiltered list when the tag matches nothing, so shared links stop dead-ending. Deliberately temporary — it silently ignores the user's intent.

timeline.title: Monitors green; incident closed as mitigated
timeline.body: All shared links render content again. The silent-fallback debt is logged for a proper empty-state decision.

timeline.title: Durable fix through the decision loop
timeline.body: interface-directions compares three real empty states against the live dataset; direction B (explain and suggest) is accepted, and STOP-01 later keeps remote suggestions out of scope. The hotfix fallback is replaced by the accepted empty state.

rootCause: renderFromUrl() had no branch between "no tag requested" and "tag requested but unknown" — both produced an empty visible list, and the second produced it silently. The spec at that revision carried no acceptance criterion for unknown tags, so nothing failed before users did.
conditions: The Gate A spec had no acceptance criterion for unknown tags — the gap shipped because nothing asserted it (criterion 3 exists now).
No fixture link with an unknown tag existed in QA notes, so the path was never walked before users walked it.
Blank-list rendering produced no signal: the page was "working" by every existing check.

followUps.text: Accepted empty state replaces the silent fallback (interface-directions, direction B)
Acceptance criterion 3 added: unknown tags must render the accepted empty state
QA sweep template gains an unknown-tag link so the path is walked every release

unresolved: whether blank-render detection is worth an automated probe at this catalog size
